gazette: Official Journal of the European Union - OJ L, 2024/745, 23.02.2024
title: EU Commission Updated Sanctions Regulation Against Russia
bodyHtml:
The EU Commission has amended Regulation No. 2014/833 regarding sanctions against Russia.

27 new companies supporting Russia's military and industrial complex have been added to List IV, which identifies individuals and firms directly supporting Russia. For the first time, one company from each of Turkey, Serbia, Thailand, India, and Sri Lanka has been added to the relevant list.

Additionally, a change has been made in Annex XXIII, which lists products prohibited for export to Russia. All items classified under tariff position 8504 (Electric transformers, static converters, rectifiers, and inductors) are now included in the relevant list.

An item classified under tariff position 8532.22, "Aluminum electrolytic fixed capacitors (excluding power capacitors)," has been added to the limited list of products that could contribute to Russia's technological development in the defense and security sector (Annex VII).

Click here for the relevant regulatory changes.
